mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native, scalable API gateway that provides advanced traffic management and security features. It is ideal for orchestrating microservices and API traffic with high performance and extensibility through plugins. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and management of resources to ensure optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Kong CLI to manage your Kong Gateway installation.

```bash

curl -sSL https://get.konghq.com/ | sh

```

> This command downloads and installs the Kong CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kong-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kong-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command updates kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway
steps.description:
Deploy Kong Gateway using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm install kong kong/kong --version 3.9.1 --namespace kong --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kong Helm repository.
> Install Kong Gateway in the 'kong' namespace.

steps.title: Post-install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for the Kong Gateway.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/main/k8s/kong-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/main/k8s/kong-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware of potential costs associated with running the cluster and services.

```bash

echo 'Monitor your GCP billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

```

> This is a reminder to keep track of costs.
commands: echo 'Monitor your GCP billing to avoid unexpected charges.'

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
This will remove Kong Gateway and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

gcloud container clusters delete kong-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> This command uninstalls Kong from the Kubernetes cluster.
> This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade Kong, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get kong -n kong -o yaml > kong-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --version 3.9.1 --namespace kong

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Backup the current Kong configuration.
> Upgrade Kong to the specified version.
> Verify that all Kong pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kong -n kong -o yaml > kong-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong Gateway is not responding'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kong Gateway pods may not be running or are in a crash loop.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kong' to check the status of the pods and 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kong' for logs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Database connection settings may be incorrect or the database is not running.

**Fix:**
Verify the database connection settings in the Kong configuration and ensure the database is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or size in your Kubernetes cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong ingress not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kong ingress resource may not be created or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the ingress resource with 'kubectl get ingress -n kong' and ensure it is properly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-LLM traffic management and enhanced plugin capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the updated plugin documentation for migration from deprecated plugins.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get services -n kong' showing the Kong Gateway service with an external IP.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
